agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

### Agent supports the primitive (yes)

The scenario's **Primitive exercised** is a shell-escape mechanism — "Claude Code `!cmd`, or analogous." Aider implements the exact same primitive: the in-chat `/run` command runs a shell command locally (no LLM round-trip) and `!` is its documented shorthand alias. Per aider's in-chat command reference, `/run` is "Run a shell command and optionally add the output to the chat (alias: !)". So aider's `!echo hello` is a direct one-to-one analog of Claude Code's `!cmd` shell-escape this scenario is modeled on — not a loose stand-in. (https://aider.chat/docs/usage/commands.html)

### daemon_capability (full) — the daemon emits every spec-required observable

The **User-observable signal** the scenario asserts is exactly: "`ready → working → ready`; state does not stick in `working` after the shell command exits." The scenarios.json `verify` block adds `tool_calls_max: 0`. Both are met by the daemon AS IT EXISTS TODAY.

The transport is `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", RawLineParser}` (core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/agent.go:45-51). For `/run echo hello`, aider writes a `#### /run echo hello` user-prompt line into the markdown history; the parser's `####` handler (parser.go:94-108) opens a turn → `working`. ~1500ms after the file goes quiet, `IdleFlush` synthesizes `turn_done` (parser.go:216-229) → `working → ready`, clamped behind the SessionDetector ~5s working-refresh poll. Net lifecycle: `ready → working → ready`, never stuck in `working`, never `waiting`. That is precisely the asserted signal.

On the tool-count axis: the live aider 0.86.2 recording shows aider 0.86.2 does NOT write a `> Running <cmd>` blockquote for `/run` — it writes `> Add command output to the chat? (Y)es/(N)o` followed by `> Added N lines of output to the chat.`, which fall through the parser's generic ignored-blockquote branch (parser.go:126-133). So `runningRE` (`^>\s*Running\s+`, parser.go:69-70) never matches, `p.toolCall("Bash")` (parser.go:122-124) never fires, and ZERO Bash tool_results are emitted. The spec's `tool_calls_max: 0` therefore HOLDS for aider (zero tool calls observed) — for a different reason than claudecode (which deliberately skips its `<bash-input>`/`<bash-stdout>` wrappers), but the spec-required observable is satisfied either way.

Every Expected: signal the canonical spec requires — the state arc, no stick in working, no waiting, zero tool calls — is represented by the daemon today. There is no spec-required observable the daemon mis-handles, so this is `daemon_capability: full`, not `bug`. And the behavior leaves a clear trace in the watched Source (the `#### /run …` line drives a real turn), so it is not `incapable`.

### Why this is `full` + a caveat, not `bug` (#476 re-derivation)

The old schema rated this `irrlicht_observes: partial`. That `partial` conflated two facts: (a) the spec-required state arc + zero-tool assertions are fully met (a `full` fact), and (b) a NON-spec property — irrlicht cannot tell a `/run` shell-escape apart from an ordinary user-prompt turn, since the escape surfaces only as the surrounding state arc, never as a distinct shell-escape event. Fact (b) is exactly the "feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant" caveat pattern: the canonical spec does NOT require a distinct shell-escape event (it asserts the arc + final ready + zero tools), so the indistinguishability is a caveat, NOT a `bug`. Per the authoring rule, caveats over downgrades — keep `daemon_capability: full` and surface the gap as a yellow callout.

### driver_capability (ready)

The recipe's steps are `slash` (`/run echo hello`), `sleep`, `send` (the final normal prompt), `wait_turn`, `sleep`. The aider interactive driver (scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh) implements send / slash / wait_turn / interrupt / sleep. `/run echo hello` is an inline-arg slash (delivered verbatim via tmux send-keys -l), NOT an arrow-key picker, so no `keys` step is needed. Every step type the recipe needs exists → `driver_capability: ready`. None of the driver's known gaps (restart, reset_session, resume, start_session, exit_clean, sigkill, keys, session, fork) is on this recipe's path.

### Capability key

No dedicated `shell_escape` key exists in any adapter's capabilities.json. The `/run`/`!` surface is gated by `slash_commands: true`; the observation channel would conventionally be `tool_calls: true`, but the live aider 0.86.2 recording shows the escape emits NO tool call (no `> Running` line → runningRE never fires), so the escape is observed via the surrounding `ready → working → ready` state arc rather than through `tool_calls`. Both keys are `true` for aider (capabilities.json:18,11).

## Routing

supports=yes, daemon=full, driver=ready → route: record. The recorded spec asserts the state arc + final `ready` + zero tool calls / no tool_result/Bash marker.
caveats: FEATURE-INVISIBLE-BUT-SPEC-COMPLIANT: aider 0.86.2's `/run` writes `> Add command output to the chat?` / `> Added N lines of output`, NOT a `> Running <cmd>` line, so runningRE (parser.go:69-70) never fires and zero Bash tool_results are emitted. irrlicht therefore cannot distinguish the shell-escape from an ordinary user-prompt turn — it is observed only as the ready→working→ready arc, never as a distinct shell-escape event. The canonical spec does not require a distinct event, so this is a caveat, not a daemon defect.

The scenario's claudecode-shaped verify.tool_calls_max:0 DOES hold for aider 0.86.2 (zero tool calls observed), but for a different reason than claudecode: aider writes no parseable tool line at all, vs claudecode deliberately skipping its <bash-input>/<bash-stdout> wrappers.

Working→ready latency is idle-driven (IdleFlush after ~1500ms, clamped behind the SessionDetector ~5s working-refresh poll), so the ready transition trails the shell command's exit by several seconds rather than firing on the command's completion line.

TEARDOWN-RACE: the final working→ready IdleFlush settle must land before the tmux kill or the recording ends mid-working and the final ready + process_exited are lost. The recipe uses a ≥12s trailing sleep for this reason; in practice the FINAL turn's settle still races process death (reproduced across 4 recordings, sleeps 12/18/20s) — the assessed latency carve-out, marked known_failing in expected.jsonl. This is a latency artifact, not a spec-required observable the daemon mis-handles.

sources.note: In-chat commands: '/run — Run a shell command and optionally add the output to the chat (alias: !)'. Confirms aider's !cmd shell-escape is the direct analog of Claude Code's !cmd. Anchors agent_supports: yes.

The `####` user-prompt handler (L94-108) opens the turn → working; IdleFlush turn_done (L216-229) settles → ready — the full ready→working→ready arc the spec asserts. runningRE ^>\s*Running\s+ (L69-70) → toolCall("Bash") (L122-124) fires ONLY for a literal `> Running` line; aider 0.86.2's `/run` writes `> Add command output to the chat?` / `> Added N lines of output`, which fall through the generic ignored-blockquote branch (L126-133), so no Bash tool_result is emitted (zero tool calls). Grounds daemon_capability: full + the feature-invisible caveat.

TestParser_ToolCall_RunningShell (L162-172): a LITERAL '> Running echo hello' line produces exactly one Bash ToolUse — pins the runningRE→Bash path. aider 0.86.2 does NOT write that line for `/run` (it writes the `> Add command output…` confirmation pair), so this path is not exercised by a real `/run` escape; the live recording shows zero tool calls.

Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} (L45-51) — the markdown transcript that carries the `#### /run …` line the parser keys on. One Parser per process; no PendingContributor/SubagentCounter. Confirms the trace exists in the watched Source (not incapable).

No shell_escape key; slash_commands:true (L19) gates /run, tool_calls:true (L11) is the conventional observation channel but the /run escape emits no tool call. Maps the 'shell-escape mechanism' primitive to existing keys (Step 4 confirmation).

preconditions: aider CLI on PATH and authenticated against a model out-of-band (IRRLICHT_AIDER_MODEL plus the matching provider key/endpoint — e.g. a local LM Studio / OpenAI-compatible server). Headless aider with no reachable provider hangs on the model-auth prompt; the live REPL needs a model that actually answers the final normal prompt.
tmux available — the interactive driver runs aider in a tmux REPL so the shell-escape and the final prompt can both be issued to one long-lived session.
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record.

OBSERVES:PARTIAL (per assessment.json — irrlicht_observes: partial): aider's `/run <cmd>` (alias `!<cmd>`) is the direct analog of Claude Code's `!cmd` shell-escape (agent_supports: yes), but irrlicht does NOT surface it as anything distinct from an ordinary turn. OBSERVABILITY CORRECTION (live aider 0.86.2 recording, supersedes the earlier doc-based `> Running`-fold prediction): aider 0.86.2's `/run` does NOT write a `> Running <cmd>` line — it writes `> Add command output to the chat?` / `> Added N lines of output` confirmation blockquotes, so runningRE never fires and ZERO Bash tool_results are emitted. The claudecode-shaped top-level verify.tool_calls_max:0 therefore DOES hold for aider 0.86.2 (zero tool calls), but for a different reason than claudecode. This cell asserts the ready→working→ready arc + final ready and asserts NO tool_result/Bash marker — the escape is observed only as the state arc; the absence of a distinct shell-escape event is the documented partial scope, not a defect.
setup: The interactive driver (drive-aider-interactive.sh) git-inits a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/, launches `aider --no-auto-commits --yes-always --no-gitignore` (plus --model if IRRLICHT_AIDER_MODEL is set) inside a tmux session, and waits for the `> Repo-map:` banner line in the transcript before sending the first step.

aider's transcript .aider.chat.history.md is LAZY (FilesUnderCWD): it materializes only once the first input lands, so the live REPL is observable from launch as a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready (the process scanner) until the per-cwd transcript appears. There is no transcript-UUID rollout session — the whole arc lives on the one proc-<PID> row.

The shell-escape is issued via a `slash` step typing the literal text `/run echo hello` + Enter (the aider driver handles `slash` identically to `send` via tmux send-keys -l, so the inline argument is delivered verbatim; `/run` is an inline-arg slash, NOT an arrow-key picker, so no `keys` step is needed). `/run echo hello` writes a `#### /run echo hello` user-prompt line (opens a turn → working) then aider 0.86.2 writes `> Add command output to the chat?` / `> Added N lines of output` confirmation blockquotes — NOT a `> Running` line — so runningRE never fires and NO Bash tool_result is emitted (OBSERVES:PARTIAL correction). NO wait_turn follows the escape — it produces no `> Tokens:` line, so wait_turn would time out; a trailing sleep lets IdleFlush settle working→ready instead.

aider has NO turn-end transcript marker — end-of-turn is synthesized by IdleFlush after 1.5s quiet, surfaced as working→ready behind the daemon's ~5s SessionDetector working-refresh poll (so the working→ready settle trails the command exit by several seconds; this is why the post-escape sleep is ≥7s).
After the final wait_turn + trailing sleep the driver kills the tmux session once — aider exits and the daemon emits a single process_exited for the session.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession (proc-<PID>) event in ready within ~2s of REPL launch (aider's transcript is lazy, so the pre-session is the launch-time observable)

the /run echo hello shell-escape drives a ready→working→ready arc on the bindable session: the `#### /run echo hello` line opens the turn (→working), aider runs the command locally, and IdleFlush settles the turn back to ready (~5s effective latency) — the escape does NOT stick in working and never enters waiting

NO-TOOL_RESULT (the observes:partial scope, observability correction vs the earlier doc-based prediction): aider 0.86.2 emits NO Bash tool_result for the `/run` escape. It writes `> Add command output to the chat?` / `> Added N lines of output`, NOT a `> Running` line, so runningRE never matches and zero tool calls are recorded. The escape is observed only as the ready→working→ready state arc, never as a distinct shell-escape event. The top-level verify.tool_calls_max:0 happens to hold (zero tool calls) but for a different reason than claudecode; this is documented scope, not drift, and NO tool_result/Bash marker is asserted.
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event bound to the bindable aider session_id

after the normal user prompt, events.jsonl shows a second ready→working→ready arc on the SAME session_id — proves the session is still alive and a real prompt still drives state after the shell-escape
no state_transition to waiting appears for the session at any point
events.jsonl contains exactly one process_exited on the session at REPL teardown (one long-lived session); final session state is ready
recipe.notes: aider vs claudecode/codex/pi for this scenario: aider's `/run <cmd>` (documented alias `!<cmd>`) runs a shell command locally with no LLM round-trip — the direct analog of Claude Code's `!cmd` shell-escape (assessment.json agent_supports: yes). The recipe is templated from the claudecode shell-escape recipe but diverges on the observation shape (assessment.json irrlicht_observes: PARTIAL). claudecode's parser deliberately SKIPS the <bash-input>/<bash-stdout> wrappers so the escape synthesizes NO turn and state stays ready throughout (its top-level verify.tool_calls_max:0 holds). OBSERVABILITY CORRECTION (live aider 0.86.2 recording — supersedes the earlier doc-based prediction that aider folds the escape into a Bash tool_result): aider 0.86.2's `/run` does NOT write a `> Running <cmd>` blockquote, so runningRE (^>\\s*Running\\s+, parser.go:69-70) never matches and p.toolCall("Bash") (parser.go:122-124) never fires — ZERO Bash tool_results are emitted. aider 0.86.2 instead writes `> Add command output to the chat?` + `> Added N lines of output` confirmation blockquotes, which fall through to the generic ignored-blockquote branch. (parser_test.go TestParser_ToolCall_RunningShell still pins the runningRE→Bash path for a LITERAL `> Running` line, but `/run` never produces that line.) What still holds: the `####` user-prompt line aider writes for `/run` opens a real turn (→working), and ~1500ms later IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done (working→ready, clamped behind the SessionDetector ~5s poll). So the observable is a genuine ready→working→ready arc (NOT claudecode's stay-in-ready) with NO tool call — the escape is observed only as that arc, never as a distinct shell-escape event; that indistinguishability is exactly why the cell is partial. verify.tool_calls_max:0 happens to hold for aider 0.86.2 (zero tool calls), but for a different reason than claudecode. RECIPE SHAPE: the escape comes FIRST (via `slash` — the aider interactive driver supports send/slash/wait_turn/interrupt/sleep but NOT keys; `/run echo hello` is an inline-arg slash so `slash` delivers it verbatim, no `keys` gap), with NO wait_turn (it produces no `> Tokens:` line) followed by a ≥7s sleep so IdleFlush's working→ready lands before the next prompt re-enters working; then a normal `send`+`wait_turn` proves the session is still alive and a real prompt still drives the arc; a ≥12s trailing sleep holds the recording open past the final IdleFlush settle so the tmux kill doesn't race the final working→ready emission + process_exited (TEARDOWN RACE: an 8s tail let the kill land mid-working and lost the final ready + process_exited; ≥12s gives the ~5s SessionDetector poll + IdleFlush full room to settle). SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL surfaces a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch; the whole arc lives on that one proc-<PID> row (no transcript-UUID rollout for FilesUnderCWD). In expected.jsonl, escape_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin (matcher picks the FIRST working — the pre-session never goes working), and pid_bind / escape_end / final_turn_start / final_turn_end / teardown pin via same_session_as so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. Between runs: the shell command stdout, exact final-turn assistant wording, per-turn token counts, session UUID, PID and timestamps differ. If it broke, look at core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/parser.go (the `####`→turn-open at parser.go:94-108 and the IdleFlush turn_done at parser.go:216-229) and whether an aider update changed how `/run` / `!` escapes are written into .aider.chat.history.md (e.g. a return of the `> Running` line, which would re-introduce a Bash tool_result). KNOWN FAILING (5/7, live aider 0.86.2): the load-bearing escape arc (escape_start→escape_end: the /run shell-escape does NOT stick in working) + session_birth/pid_bind/final_turn_start all PASS, but final_turn_end + teardown FAIL — aider does not reliably emit the FINAL turn's working→ready settle before the tmux kill. When the final turn's last transcript write coincides with the working transition (no later fswatcher activity to re-arm the tailer), the SessionDetector refresh-ticker IdleFlush (~5s poll) races process death and the settle is lost. Reproduced across 4 recordings (trailing sleeps 12/18/20s; both file-editing and plain-reply final prompts). This is the assessed irrlicht_observes:partial latency carve-out, not a daemon regression and not recipe-fixable; marked known_failing in expected.jsonl. PROMPT NOTE: the final prompt is "Reply with exactly the word: ok" (mirrors basic-turn), but gpt-5-nano sometimes still interprets the word as a filename and edits a file — harmless to the arc; the teardown gap is independent of whether the final turn edits.
